reasoning:
The base value on or near 2025-03-30 corresponds to a recent sharp spike in bank MBS holdings (hitting 2668-2670 in mid-to-late March 2025, up from ~2626 just five weeks prior). Given the typical mean-reverting behavior of commercial bank balance sheet data around a secular trend, a value this far above the 6-month average (~2647) is statistically unlikely to be exceeded in the immediate following weeks. The FRED trend analysis tool estimates only a 0.27 to 0.30 probability of staying above 2669 for the nearest resolution date (April 6). 

As the forecast horizon increases, the variance (uncertainty) of the time series projection increases. Even if the expected mean reverts to around 2650–2660, the widening confidence intervals over 1-month, 3-month, 6-month, and 1-year horizons mean that a growing portion of the probability distribution will exceed the 2669 threshold, systematically pulling the probability closer to 0.5. Therefore, probabilities start low to reflect immediate mean reversion, and increase monotonically toward 0.5 to reflect rising longer-term uncertainty.
